claim_status: No-claim incident-response source observations only; these sources are not a Turing incident program, severity policy, disclosure authority, emergency patch result, supported-security decision, production evidence, or readiness approval.

sources.title: Incident Response Recommendations and Considerations for Cybersecurity Risk Management, SP 800-61 Revision 3
sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.observation: NIST SP 800-61r3 integrates incident response with cybersecurity risk management and addresses preparation, detection, response, recovery, communications, and improvement.
sources.decision_consequence: A Turing rehearsal must preserve lifecycle state, evidence, authority, communications, recovery, and lessons learned as separate records; NIST does not select staffing, severity targets, disclosure dates, signing, or supported-version policy.

sources.title: NIST Incident Response project
sources.publisher: National Institute of Standards and Technology
sources.observation: NIST identifies SP 800-61 Revision 3 as the current incident-response publication and provides the project context for applying incident-response guidance.
sources.decision_consequence: The documentation must record the exact guidance revision and retrieval context rather than relying on an undated incident process; the project page does not prove Turing's operational capability or authority.

sources.title: CVSS v4.0 Specification Document
sources.observation: CVSS v4.0 separates Base, Threat, Environmental, and Supplemental metric groups, with vectors preserving the metrics used and consumer context affecting interpretation.
sources.decision_consequence: Triage records must retain the CVSS version, vector, score, scorer, timestamp, threat and environmental context, and uncertainty; a score cannot by itself authorize containment, disclosure, signing, promotion, or closure.

sources.title: CVSS v4.0 Consumer Implementation Guide
sources.observation: The CVSS consumer guidance explains how threat and environmental information enriches a base assessment for a specific deployment and how the result remains an input to broader risk management.
sources.decision_consequence: A Turing incident record must keep deployment-specific impact, mitigations, exploitation state, and other business or safety factors visible outside the numeric score; missing context remains an unresolved decision input.

sources.title: Information Sharing and Coordinated Vulnerability Disclosure
sources.observation: CISA describes coordinated vulnerability disclosure as coordination of remediation and public disclosure between a reporter and affected vendors.
sources.decision_consequence: Future rehearsal records must separate private intake, remediation coordination, affected-party communication, public disclosure, credit, embargo, and legal review; CISA does not grant Turing disclosure authority or define every jurisdiction's obligations.

sources.title: Vulnerability Disclosure Policy Platform fact sheet
sources.observation: CISA's vulnerability-disclosure-policy material describes a structured mechanism intended to support good-faith security research and coordinated disclosure.
sources.decision_consequence: A future Turing policy must explicitly define intake scope, researcher communication, safe handling, escalation, and public-disclosure boundaries; the CISA platform material is not a Turing policy, legal determination, or executed intake capability.

unsupported: No Turing private intake, incident command process, severity policy, disclosure policy, emergency patch capacity, or supported-security policy has been approved or executed.
No private tabletop, emergency patch dry run, regression/backport result, signing/update rehearsal, coordinated disclosure rehearsal, postmortem, or owner-reviewed PB-018 readiness decision exists.
No production signing material, live vulnerability, unreduced exploit details, real user data, credentials, or private-session contents are used by this manifest.

No incident-response readiness, disclosure authority, signing authority, stable promotion, supported-security, production, Chrome-class, performance, or broad implementation claim follows from these sources.
